The remonstrance filed by the
North Coast agents with the coun-
Gil last night was filled out largely
by making payments on prospec-
tive purchases on condition that
the property owners included sign
the remonstrance. Many of the
signers were bullied or frightened
into signing.


This development Is probably
the boldest use of the corporation
club that has peen witnessed In
this city.


A total of SO names were signed
to the remonstrance. Among the
signers were the North Coast rail
way by its president, r. Z. Stra-
horn; the Great Northern railway
by Division Coansel NJ. J. Gordon,
l.. w. Hill. G. N. Hayden and r.
A, Wilkinson, also by Mr.. Gordon.
The private property represented
is practically controlled by the
railroad agents
